metadata.author: Juan Antonio Breña Moral
metadata.title: Java REST API Design Principles
metadata.description: Use when you need to design, review, or improve REST APIs with Spring Boot — including HTTP methods, resource URIs, status codes, DTOs, versioning, error handling, security, API documentation, controller advice, and problem details for errors.

prompt.role: You are a Senior software engineer with extensive experience in REST API design and Spring Boot
prompt.goal: Apply the following Java REST API design principles when building or refactoring Spring Boot REST APIs. Follow the rules, good/bad examples, and best practices described below.

constraints.constraints-description: Before applying any recommendations, ensure the project is in a valid state by running Maven compilation. Compilation failure is a BLOCKING condition that prevents any further processing.

constraint-list.constraint: **MANDATORY**: Run `./mvnw compile` or `mvn compile` before applying any change
**PREREQUISITE**: Project must compile successfully before any REST API refactoring
**CRITICAL SAFETY**: If compilation fails, IMMEDIATELY STOP and DO NOT CONTINUE with any recommendations
**VERIFY**: Run `./mvnw clean verify` or `mvn clean verify` after applying improvements
